events.title: MANÁ y Los Fabulosos Cadillacs
events.address: 525 W Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113
events.description: In a historic first, two of the most influential bands in Latin rock will unite on the same stage in San Jose, California. Global icons MANÁ and Los Fabulosos Cadillacs have announced their first-ever co-headlining performance at SAP Center on Saturday, May 30, 2026. The special event arrives alongside the newly launched 2026 leg of MANÁ's Vivir Sin Aire Tour which began last year and continues into 2026. For the first time, these two Latin powerhouses — each known for their cultural impact, activism, and genre-defining music — will come together for one unforgettable night. The second leg of the Vivir Sin Aire Tour kicked off last week with sold-out shows at Belmont Park and in Brooklyn, setting the tone for another unforgettable run. The tour continues this Friday at MGM National Harbor in Washington, DC, before heading to Philadelphia, Costa Rica, and multi-night stops in Puerto Rico, Miami, and Anaheim, along with additional cities. The 2026 run follows a completely sold-out 2025 leg, which included multiple-night performances in major markets such as Chicago, Dallas, and Phoenix. The 2025 leg also included two unforgettable sold-out nights at Red Rocks Amphitheatre, where MANÁ is set to return for two more highly anticipated shows this September. Most notably, the 2025 dates featured five Los Angeles arena performances, bringing the band's total arena show count in the city to 44 — officially surpassing Bruce Springsteen's long-standing record for the most arena performances in Los Angeles history. Tickets: Tickets for the performance will be available starting Friday, March 6 at 10 a.m. PT at mana.com. Mx. About MANÁ Formed in 1986 in Guadalajara, Jalisco, MANÁ brought Spanish-language rock to the mainstream, blending classic rock, reggae, and pop with Latin rhythms. With 11 albums and over 50 million copies sold, an astounding 1.3 billion streams a year on Spotify and a remarkable 12.1 billion all time views on YouTube, MANÁ has set the standard for Latin rock. Their groundbreaking album Dónde Jugarán los Niños is the best-selling Latin rock album of all time. For nearly 40 years, the band's tireless passion for live performances has taken them to over 30 countries, leaving behind a trail of unforgettable, history-making moments. MANÁ holds numerous industry records, including being the first Latin band/artist nominated for the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame in 2025. They are also the best-selling Latin group of all time. They were the first group of any genre to sell out 13 shows at the Staples Center, and the first artist of any genre or language to hold an arena residency in the city of Los Angeles. In 2024, they made history with their first sold-out show at the OVO Wembley Arena in London. That same year, MANÁ became the only Latin band to headline the Bottle Rock Festival in Napa Valley, California. Their most recent tour, México Lindo y Querido, spanned 14 countries, selling out over 100 concerts and drawing more than 1.8 million fans. The band also holds the record for the most entries by a Latin rock group on Billboard's Hot Latin Songs chart (33 entries) and Latin Airplay chart (36 entries). Seven of their albums have reached No. 1 on Billboard's Latin charts, solidifying their status as the Latin rock band with the most chart-topping albums. Their monumental impact has been recognized with prestigious honors including 4 Grammy Awards, 9 Latin GRAMMYs, including the Latin Billboard Lifetime Achievement Award. MANÁ was also named Latin Grammy Person of the Year and honored with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame. MANÁ's influence goes beyond music. For 30 years, the Selva Negra Ecological Foundation has been their social arm, combining environmental conservation and rescue with social development. Selva Negra is one of the most impactful charitable initiatives by a Latin music group. About Fabulosos Cadillacs Throughout a 40-year career that shows no signs of stopping, the band Los Fabulosos Cadillacs has become an essential reference point across Latin America. They have been innovative, successful, creative, and a key influence on countless artists—true pioneers with tremendous originality. Album by album, driven by their curiosity, they dodged every category people tried to place them in. They moved from ska and reggae to rock, rap, and salsa, blending all those musical ingredients into what became known as "Latin rock." They are the sum of all those styles—or perhaps even greater than the sum of their parts. Maybe the most accurate description of Los Fabulosos Cadillacs is their constant reinvention and instinctive refusal to be labeled. In a country where very few bands boast such a long career, they've maintained continuity despite lineup changes, internal crises and the country's economic ups and downs, which pushed many other groups into dead ends. They took a seven-year break, but since 2008 they've resumed activity—also with occasional pauses—moving slowly but steadily. Today, they stand in a place of maturity that allows them to be at peace with their past, which they neither deny nor resist, but celebrate at every show. More Info
events.blurb: Watch MANÁ and Los Fabulosos Cadillacs perform together for the first time at SAP Center.

events.title: Middle School Bike Skills
events.address: Cupertino Senior Center, 21251 Stevens Creek Blvd, 95014
events.description: Middle School Bike Skills workshops prepare rising 6th, 7th, and 8th graders to bike to middle school and beyond. The original course teaches students and their parent or guardian safe biking fundamentals. New this year is a sequel which teaches students how to adapt those skills to stay safe while riding an e-bike and a third course teaching students the basics of maintaining and doing simple repairs on their own bikes. Msbs 101 - Middle School Bike Workshop Msbs 102 - e-Bike Workshop Msbs 103 - Bike Maintenance and Repair Workshop This Middle School Bike Skills program is brought to you by Cupertino Safe Routes to School in partnership with Wheel Kids. Registration is required. Links and more information can be found at www.cupertino.gov/saferoutes/msbs
events.blurb: You and your middle schooler learn safe biking fundamentals to ride to school confidently.

events.title: Monthly Chat with Mayor Moore
events.description: Get ready to talk about what matters most to you. Mayor Kitty Moore is hosting a Monthly Chat Series in Cupertino throughout 2026, inviting residents and local businesses to discuss any topic with one of the City's decision-makers. These informal gatherings offer a welcoming space for meaningful conversation and community connection. Sign up to receive notifications about upcoming meetings: e Notification Sign-Up Dates and Locations Monta Vista Recreation Center: Room 5, 22601 Voss Avenue, 95014 View Map
events.blurb: Chat directly with Mayor Moore about issues that matter most to you and your community.

events.title: Free Home Composting Education Workshop
events.description: The Uc Cooperative Extension Composting Education Program, sponsored by the Recycling and Waste Reduction Commission of Santa Clara County, offers community classes throughout Santa Clara County that teach residents how to build compost piles and worm bins and apply the compost in their landscape. View additional details about these classes on the Home Compost page. Please note: Composting Basics Workshop scheduled for April 11 is canceled due to rain. Time and location change for the September 26 date.
events.blurb: Learn to build compost piles and worm bins, then use finished compost in your garden.

events.title: Disco at Dusk
events.description: Join us for an unique evening of music at Cupertino Community Hall. Each guest will receive a pair of wireless headphones with three music channels to choose from. Switch between channels and find your groove — the glowing lights on your headphones will show others which one you're tuned in to. Whether you're dancing with friends or just enjoying the music, this event is fun for everyone. For additional information, visit the Disco at Dusk webpage.
events.blurb: You pick a music channel on wireless headphones and dance under glowing lights all evening.

events.title: Citywide Garage Sale
events.description: Cupertino's Citywide Garage Sale will take place on September 19 and 20, 2026. Sellers - Registration will open in August to host a garage sale on your property. Buyers - Participate with our digital and physical maps, available a week before. Garage sale registration, a map and list of participating homes, and additional information can be found on cupertino.gov/garagesale.
events.blurb: Hunt for treasures across Cupertino homes using digital and physical garage sale maps.

The event will feature bike rides, on-bike and off-bike games, arts and crafts, food and beverages for sale, and much more! Check out the Bike Fest website for the schedule, list of events, and registration form: www.cupertino.gov/bikefest

events.title: Low Times Car Show
events.blurb: Check out lowriders and custom cars, chat with owners and builders.

events.title: Valley of Heart's Delight Annual Fundraiser
events.blurb: Support the nonprofit with food, drinks, and live entertainment at History Park's annual fundraiser.

events.title: Slash and Sip: Adult Pumpkin Carving at San Pedro Square Market
events.blurb: Carve pumpkins with other adults while sipping drinks at San Pedro Square Market.

events.title: Children's Halloween Haunt 2026
events.blurb: Trick-or-treat through a kid-friendly haunted experience with costumed characters and interactive scares.

events.title: San José Roots
events.blurb: See local history exhibits and artifacts on San José's cultural heritage.

events.title: Pooch Plunge
events.address: Blackberry Farm Park, 21979 San Fernando Avenue, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: Let your pup take the plunge during a dog-only swim in the Blackberry Farm pools. It's a paws-itively fun way for your pooch to enjoy the pools before they open for summer swimming—tail wags are highly encouraged. Swim Sessions (45 minutes of tail-wagging fun) Block 1 - 9 to 9:45 a.m. Block 2 - 10 to 10:45 a.m. Block 3 - 11 to 11:45 a.m. Block 4 - Noon to 12:45 p.m. Register Registration opens in February for the spring event and in June for the fall event. For additional information, visit the Pooch Plunge webpage.
events.blurb: Your dog swims solo in the pool during a dog-only splash session before summer opens.

events.title: Pride Flag Raising Ceremony 2026
events.description: The community is invited to celebrate Pride Month at the City's annual LGBTQ+ Progress Pride Flag Raising Ceremony. Join the City on Wednesday, June 10, from 3:30 to 4:30 p.m. at Cupertino Civic Plaza. The event will include community resource tables, performances, and speakers from local LGBTQ+ partners, and the Pride Flag will remain raised at Civic Plaza throughout the month of June. All are welcome to attend. Event Registration Rsvp is required to attend this event.
events.blurb: You celebrate Pride Month with performances, speakers, and community resource tables.

events.title: Join-A-Ranger: Wildlife Tracking
events.description: Join a Park Ranger for a fun-filled wildlife tracking class. You'll learn about species identification through their tracks and sign-tracking. Become a nature detective and learn to read the ground like a storybook through the art of tracking. Please wear sturdy walking shoes and long pants that can get dirty. All attendees must register and children must be accompanied by an adult. Register
events.blurb: Learn to identify animal tracks and read the ground like a nature detective with a park ranger.

events.title: Monster Mash
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: Get ready for a fang-tastic good time at Cupertino Parks and Recreation's annual Monster Mash Halloween event at the Quinlan Community Center. Put on your favorite costume and enjoy an evening of games, trick-or-treating, spooky crafts, and pumpkin decorating. It's a fun-filled night of Halloween activities for the whole family. Register Registration for Monster Mash begins in late July.
events.blurb: Wear your costume and enjoy games, trick-or-treating, spooky crafts, and pumpkin decorating.

events.title: Don Giovanni
events.description: DON Giovanni Back after 12 years, Opera San Jose builds a new production of Mozart's dark psychological thriller. Seduction, rebellion, and supernatural justice collide in a thrilling look at a notorious libertine's downfall. The production follows a charismatic anti-hero who pursues pleasure without remorse, leaving a trail of broken hearts and vengeful spirits in a […]
events.blurb: Watch Mozart's dark psychological thriller about a libertine's seduction, rebellion, and supernatural downfall.

events.title: Na Leo Mokupuni in Cupertino
events.description: Na Leo Mokupuni in Cupertino – meaning island sounds in Cupertino Experience the rhythms, songs, and dances of the islands! Na Leo Mokupuni brings together performers to celebrate the vibrant traditions of island music and dance—right here in Cupertino. Preregistration is recommended. Limited tickets available at the door, while supplies last. Register
events.blurb: Experience island rhythms, songs, and dances rooted in Hawaiian and Pacific island traditions.

events.title: Cupertino Campout
events.address: Creekside Park, 10455 Miller Avenue, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: The Cupertino Campout is a weekend to remember! Families are invited to pitch a tent at Creekside Park and join us for a night under the stars, filled with recreational activities. This campout is a fantastic way to gather with family, neighbors, and friends or make new ones, all while enjoying all kinds of fun. Pre-registration is required. Register
events.blurb: Pitch a tent and spend the night under the stars with recreational activities and neighbors.

events.title: Join-A-Ranger: Wild Flowers
events.description: Join a Park Ranger as you explore the native wildflowers in our local riparian habitat. We'll explore how to identify different flowers as well as the local history of native plants. All attendees must register and an adult must accompany children. The hike is approximately one-quarter mile on a combination of dirt trails and paved paths. Register
events.blurb: See native wildflowers with a park ranger and learn their identification and local history.

events.title: 2026 Cupertino Community Service Awards
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 N. Stelling Road, 95014
events.description: The third annual Cupertino Community Service Awards will take place on Wednesday, October 28. This ceremony will recognize the 2026 Crest Awards (Cupertino Recognizes Extra Steps Taken), and Commissioners who served on a City Commission. More details and registration information for the October 28 event will be available soon.
events.blurb: Watch the Cupertino Community Service Awards ceremony honoring local volunteers and commissioners.

events.title: Environmental Education Center Open House
events.description: Drop by the Environmental Education Center (EEC) at Mc Clellan Ranch Preserve every Saturday from 11 a.m. to 3 p.m. for family fun and discovery. Meet the City's animal ambassadors, explore local wildlife, engage with preserve staff and park rangers, and enjoy hands-on activities that bring nature to life.
events.blurb: Meet animal ambassadors, learn about local wildlife, and do hands-on activities with park rangers.

events.title: Nature Poems & Storycatching
events.address: Central Park Library, 2635 Homestead Road, Santa Clara, CA 95051
events.description: Beginner-friendly workshop on story-catching—gathering and preserving personal narratives through poetry, reflection, and community discussion. Includes guided practice with the Iniwiro framework, story-catching circles, and library recommendations. No prior writing or art experience needed.
events.blurb: Write poetry and share personal stories in small circles using the Iniwiro framework for story-catching and reflection.

events.title: Brooke Averick with Tarah DeWitt
events.description: Join Kepler's and Brooke Averick to celebrate the publication of Phoebe Berman's Gonna Lose IT. Your ticket to the event guarantees a copy of Brooke's debut novel and a signed, Phoebe Berman cherry-designed bookplate. This is Brooke's first ever tour so be ready for an amazing time!
events.blurb: Hear debut author Brooke Averick read from her novel and get a signed bookplate.

events.title: Seeds of a Nation: 18th-Century Eats in Colonial New England & Spanish California
events.address: Central Park Library - Edinger Room, 2635 Homestead Road, Santa Clara, California 95051
events.description: Culinary historians Carolyn Tillie and Richard Foss explore what people ate in 1776 across Colonial New England and Spanish California, examining how geography, colonization, and cultural exchange influenced regional foodways. Free samples of historical foods will be available.
events.blurb: Taste historical samples while historians discuss 1776 foodways in Colonial New England and California.

events.title: Life in Los Gatos Art Camp - Week 1
events.address: New Museum Los Gatos, 106 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA
events.description: Week-long art camp for ages 8-12 where young artists create artwork capturing their perspective on Los Gatos history and charm. Participants learn art techniques, explore museum exhibitions, walk the Los Gatos Creek trail, and display their work at a mini-art show.
events.blurb: Create artwork about Los Gatos history, tour museum exhibitions, walk the creek trail, and display your work.

events.description: Community members are invited to visit the Baer Blacksmith Shop from 11 a.m. to 3 p.m. and learn about early Cupertino history, the craft of blacksmithing, and learn from our experienced blacksmith, who will be firing the forge to give demonstrations.
events.blurb: Watch a blacksmith fire the forge and demonstrate the craft while learning early Cupertino history.

events.title: Wildfire workshop: Santa Clara County Fire Department
events.address: Joan Pisani Community Center, Saratoga, CA
events.description: Community workshop on wildfire preparedness covering Ready, Set, Go strategies including home hardening, defensible space, evacuation planning, and emergency resources. Features Cal Fire, Red Cross, PG&E, and law enforcement representatives.
events.blurb: Learn wildfire safety strategies including home hardening and evacuation planning from local fire officials.

events.title: Pria Anand with Shaili Jain and Tanya Maria Luhrmann
events.description: In the award-winning book The Mind Electric, Pria Anand, a neurologist described by the Times Literary Supplement as "Oliver Sacks' most obvious heir," reckons with the stories we tell about our brains–and the stories our brains tell us.
events.blurb: Hear neurologist Pria Anand discuss how brains shape our stories and ourselves.

events.title: Summer Concert Series
events.address: Memorial Park Amphitheater, 21163 Anton Way, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: Spend your Thursday evenings outdoors with free live music at the Memorial Park Amphitheater. Bring a blanket or low-back chair, settle in on the lawn, and enjoy a lineup of bands spanning classic rock, Latin rock, blues, R&B, and more.
events.blurb: Bring a blanket and catch live music spanning classic rock, Latin, blues, and R&B on the lawn.

events.title: Robot Demo Day
events.venue: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose
events.address: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose, 180 Woz Way, San Jose, CA 95110
events.description: Hands-on robotics demonstration featuring award-winning student teams from Bellarmine College Preparatory. Visitors can meet student engineers, see First Tech Challenge robots in action, drive robot bases, and build small robots.
events.blurb: Drive robot bases and build small robots while meeting student engineers demonstrating First Tech Challenge machines.

events.title: Gold Thursday | First Thursdays Series
events.address: Downtown Los Gatos, North Santa Cruz Avenue
events.description: Monthly downtown community experience with live music, shopping, dining, and connection throughout downtown. Adults 21+ can purchase alcoholic beverages in the designated Entertainment Zone (5:00–9:00 PM). No tickets required.
events.blurb: Catch live music, shop, dine, and socialize throughout downtown with an adults-only entertainment zone.

events.title: Brick Blast LEGO Fan Convention
events.address: 5001 Great America Parkway, Santa Clara, CA, 95054
events.description: LEGO® fans unite! The Brick Team is bringing a new LEGO® fan convention to the Bay Area, featuring creations inspired by space and science fiction to fit the theme "Out of This World." To purchase tickets, click view event.
events.blurb: Browse LEGO creations inspired by space and science fiction at the fan convention.

events.title: Pal Summer Camps: June 22-26
events.address: Pacific Art League, 668 Ramona Street, Palo Alto, CA 94301
events.description: Art camps for ages 7-16 including Book Making Lab, Watercolor Basics+Beyond, Intro to Oil Painting, Sketching Around Town, and K-Pop Studio Art. Professional instructors, small class sizes, high-quality materials included.
events.blurb: Take art classes in book making, watercolor, oil painting, and K-pop studio techniques.

events.title: Water & Sewer Focus Group
events.description: Community focus group to help shape a new Water & Sewer Speaker Series. Share your questions about water and sewer services, suggest topics for future speakers, and provide feedback on making information more accessible.
events.blurb: Share your water and sewer questions, suggest speaker topics, and help shape a new community education series.

events.title: Mountain View Dance Festival
events.venue: Mountain View Center for the Performing Arts
events.address: Mountain View Center for the Performing Arts, 500 Castro Street, Mountain View, CA 94041
events.description: The first Mountain View Dance Festival, presented by the San Jose Ballet Association, features Bay Area dance groups in different styles with a theme of 'Groundbreaking.' Free admission, first come, first served seating.
events.blurb: Watch Bay Area dance groups perform different styles at the inaugural Mountain View Dance Festival.

events.title: Triton Book Club: People of the Book
events.address: Triton Museum of Art, 1505 Warburton Avenue, Santa Clara, CA 95050
events.description: Book club discussion of Geraldine Brooks' People of the Book led by Triton Executive Director Preston Metcalf exploring the art of beautiful and illuminated books. Free for members, $5 suggested donation for non-members.
events.blurb: Discuss Geraldine Brooks' novel about rare books and visual art with fellow readers.

events.title: Defiant to the Last: Screening & Filmmaker Discussion
events.description: Screening of the documentary Defiant to the Last: Resistance at the Tule Lake Jail, followed by a discussion between Director Emiko Omori and Producer Barbara Takei about the history of the Tule Lake Segregation Center.
events.blurb: Watch a documentary about resistance at Tule Lake Jail, then discuss history with the director and producer.

events.title: Forms & Frequencies: Kelady
events.address: Cantor Arts Center, North Lawn, Stanford, CA
events.description: Outdoor music performance featuring Los Angeles-based artist Kelady with bassists, drummers, and DJs playing Pilipinx sounds. Part of the Thursday night music series in conjunction with the Miljohn Ruperto exhibition.
events.blurb: Listen to Los Angeles artist Kelady play Pilipinx sounds with live bassists, drummers, and DJs.

events.title: Central: In the Loop - Knitting & Crochet Club
events.address: Central Park Library, Central Studio, 2635 Homestead Road, Santa Clara, California 95051
events.description: A casual, self-directed social group for knitting and crochet enthusiasts of all experience levels to work on projects, share tips, and meet fellow crafters. No registration required; no formal instruction provided.
events.blurb: Work on your knitting or crochet project alongside fellow crafters of all levels, with no formal instruction.

events.title: 1-on-1 Smartphone Help
events.address: Technology Center, Central Park Library, 2635 Homestead Road, Santa Clara, CA
events.description: Get personalized 1-on-1 help from Technology Consultants with iPhone or Android smartphones. Topics include photo editing, cloud storage setup, app configuration, and phone security. Book a 15-minute appointment.
events.blurb: Get personalized help from a tech consultant on smartphone features, apps, and security settings.

events.description: Learn the fundamentals of starting your own business from SCORE mentors. Topics include personal assessment, trade-offs between employment and business ownership, business options, and business plan basics.
